decisions.decision: Implement the aggregate-status helper in `backend/server.py` against the planned `/v1/health.components` mapping contract, where each component value exposes a service-health `status`, instead of backfilling the missing step-2 component builders here or exposing a second public API over raw status iterables. If an iterable-based reduction loop is useful, keep it private to the helper.
alternatives_rejected: Backfill the missing shared `db`/`inference`/`auth` builders in this child task.
Make the public helper accept only a raw iterable of statuses and force later `/v1/health` assembly to peel nested component objects apart route-locally.
Expose both a mapping-based and iterable-based public helper even though the current codebase only needs the mapping contract.

decisions.decision: Keep the reducer strict to the `/v1/health` vocabulary `ok | degraded | unhealthy` and have it raise `ValueError` for an empty component set, a missing component `status`, or any unexpected status string so dropped components and cross-domain status leaks fail fast instead of silently producing misleading service health.
alternatives_rejected: Normalize unrelated status words such as account-capacity `healthy` inside this helper.
Default an empty component set to `ok`.
Silently coerce unexpected inputs to `degraded` or `unhealthy`.

decisions.decision: Add focused helper-level tests in `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` that inject synthetic component objects to prove the precedence order `unhealthy > degraded > ok` and the strict failure cases now, rather than waiting for later `/v1/health` route tests that can only observe all-`ok` builders in this checkout.
alternatives_rejected: Rely only on later route-level `/v1/health` coverage to catch precedence regressions indirectly.
Skip non-`ok` reducer tests until the component builders eventually emit degraded or unhealthy states in a different branch.

steps.task: Add a shared aggregate-status helper near the existing health helpers in `backend/server.py`, freezing its public input contract to a `components` mapping whose values contain `status` and optionally factoring any raw-status scan into a private inner helper.

Implement strict reduction and validation so the helper returns `unhealthy` if any component is unhealthy, otherwise `degraded` if any component is degraded, otherwise `ok`, and raises `ValueError` for empty input, missing `status`, or any unexpected service-health status.

Extend `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py` with focused helper-level coverage that injects synthetic component mappings to assert the all-`ok` case, degraded precedence, unhealthy precedence, and failure cases for empty input and invalid status values.

Run the targeted server-helper tests for the new aggregate helper and confirm the slice stays scoped to reusable health helper behavior only: no component-builder backfill, no `/v1/health` payload assembly, and no `/health` or `/healthz` route changes in this child.
